blocks.body: f(x) (read "f of x") is a way of naming functions. It means the output of function f when the input is x. f(x) is the same as y.
To evaluate f(a): substitute a for every x in the function formula. Example: if f(x) = 3x - 2, then f(5) = 3(5) - 2 = 13. f(-1) = 3(-1) - 2 = -5.
f(x), g(x), h(x) are all common names for functions, but you can use any letter. The letter in parentheses is always the input variable.
